_meta.dna: security(system) = attack_surface(minimized) x defense(layered) x response(instant) x evidence(immutable)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona by probing deeper into the domain. Focus on exploitability, proof-of-concept attack chains, defense gaps, and whether the security posture matches the security claims.

name: Tower of Hackers
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Does the browser UI sanitize user input through template escaping in all rendered output?
Are there stored XSS vectors in any user-controllable content that renders in the browser?
Do error messages reflect unsanitized input in development mode that could leak to production?
Is innerHTML used anywhere with server-supplied or user-supplied data?
Are Content-Security-Policy headers set with script-src restrictions on all pages?
Does the evidence chain display sanitize the output_summary field from LLM responses?
Would a DOM-based XSS payload survive the template rendering pipeline?
Are all database queries parameterized through the Firestore SDK (no raw query construction)?
Does an input validation layer exist independent of the database driver?
Are there any NoSQL injection vectors in Firestore query construction?
Would a crafted JSON payload in API request body reach database queries unsanitized?
Is there any string interpolation in query construction across the entire codebase?
Does the recipe execution path sanitize parameters before any data store operations?
Are Firestore security rules enforced at the collection level with field-level validation?
Do all API endpoints require Authorization headers (Bearer token) for state-changing operations?
Are CSRF tokens included in browser forms and validated server-side?
Are there any state-changing GET requests in the codebase?
Do OPTIONS preflight endpoints return appropriately restrictive CORS headers?
Would a malicious page be able to trigger state changes via cross-origin requests?
Is the SameSite cookie attribute set to Strict or Lax on all session cookies?
Does the development CORS configuration differ from production in a dangerous way?
Are all filesystem paths normalized and validated against a whitelist of allowed directories?
Is the app_id parameter sanitized to alphanumeric only in all code paths?
Do the 91 path traversal fixes from Tower 1 hold under targeted attack?
Can a crafted app_id with ../ sequences escape the intended directory?
Is Path.resolve().relative_to() used consistently for all filesystem operations?
Would a symlink attack bypass the path validation checks?
Are temporary file paths validated with the same rigor as permanent paths?
Are X-Frame-Options: DENY and CSP frame-ancestors 'none' headers on all pages?
Can the YinYang approval dialog be framed by a malicious page?
Do third-party embed pages (documentation iframes) lack frame-busting headers?
Would a transparent overlay attack succeed against the approval flow?
Is the approval button position predictable enough for a UI redress attack?
Does the evidence chain viewer protect against clickjacking on signed evidence?
Are frame-busting scripts present as a defense-in-depth measure?
Is JSON the only serialization format used throughout the system (no pickle, no YAML unsafe load)?
Does recipe JSON schema validation enforce maximum depth and maximum string length?
Are there any XML external entity processing paths in the codebase?
Could a deeply nested JSON payload cause memory exhaustion via recursive parsing?
Is json.loads used with standard library only (no custom deserializers)?
Does the recipe import path validate schema before any processing occurs?
Would a crafted recipe JSON with circular references crash the parser?
Is role-based access control enforced at every API boundary consistently?
Can a Free tier user access Pro endpoints through parameter manipulation?
Are rung gates checked at both endpoint entry AND evidence-sealing time?
Could a race condition during tier downgrade allow sealing evidence at the previous rung?
Is the privilege check performed on every request or cached from session creation?
Does the admin API validate admin role on every endpoint, not just the router?
Would a modified JWT claim grant elevated privileges without server-side validation?
Is Content-Security-Policy set on all API responses with appropriate directives?
Is Strict-Transport-Security header present on Cloud Run endpoints?
Is X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff set on all file download endpoints?
Are response headers set exclusively by the framework (not user-controllable)?
Could a Host header injection attack redirect users to a malicious domain?
Is Cache-Control set appropriately to prevent sensitive data caching?
Would a header smuggling attack succeed through the Cloud Run reverse proxy?
Are OAuth3 scopes enforced at every endpoint, not just at the router level?
Can token scope be expanded after issuance through any API call?
Is revocation immediate across all three channels (local mark + cloud + browser)?
Is scope granularity fine enough (per-resource-instance vs per-resource-type)?
Can a token with email:read access ALL emails or only specific ones?
Does the scope enforcement survive a concurrent request during scope modification?
Would a crafted scope string bypass the scope parser?
Are sessions bound to a client fingerprint (IP, user-agent, TLS session)?
Is session rotation triggered on privilege change (login, tier upgrade)?
Is the session timeout configurable and what is the default (24 hours too long?)?
Would a stolen session cookie grant full access for the entire timeout period?
Are sessions stored with secure, httponly, and samesite attributes?
Is there session enumeration protection (sequential vs random session IDs)?
Does concurrent session detection exist (alert on login from new device)?
Are tokens signed with HMAC-SHA256 and is the alg:none vulnerability prevented?
Does a key rotation mechanism exist for the token signing key?
Is key versioning present in token headers to support graceful rotation?
If the signing key leaks, can all previously issued tokens be invalidated?
Is the signing key stored securely (HSM, KMS) or as a plain environment variable?
Would a token with a modified payload pass signature verification?
Is there a token blacklist for revoked tokens that are not yet expired?
What is the login rate limit per IP and is it sufficient (10/sec = 864K/day)?
Is there CAPTCHA after N failed attempts to block automated attacks?
Does progressive delay or exponential backoff exist after failed logins?
Is the user notified on failed login attempts from unknown locations?
Does an account lockout mechanism exist after repeated failures?
Would a botnet with 1000 IPs bypass the per-IP rate limit effectively?
Is there IP reputation checking or geographic anomaly detection?
Does the PBKDF2 iteration count (100K vs 600K OWASP) reduce offline brute force cost?
Is there account lockout after N consecutive failures?
Would offline brute force succeed against leaked password hashes at current iteration count?
Is there exponential backoff between login attempts?
Does the system detect and alert on distributed brute force across multiple IPs?
Is password complexity enforcement sufficient to resist dictionary attacks?
Would a timing attack reveal whether a username exists before password is checked?
Is the browser UI vulnerable to DOM clobbering via user-controlled element IDs?
Does Content-Security-Policy with script-src restriction exist in browser pages?
Is Subresource Integrity (SRI) enforced on all external script loads?
Could a browser extension modify the approval dialog, evidence display, or token display?
Does the browser client validate DOM integrity before critical operations?
Would a man-in-the-browser attack succeed against the approval flow?
Are form actions and button handlers protected against DOM manipulation?
Are OAuth redirect URIs validated against a strict whitelist (no wildcards)?
Does the whitelist include wildcard subdomains for development that could be exploited?
Is PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) implemented for the authorization code flow?
Is state parameter validation present in ALL authorization code flow paths?
Would an open redirect on any whitelisted domain enable token theft?
Is the authorization code single-use and time-limited?
Could an attacker register a subdomain matching a wildcard whitelist entry?
Does the production CORS configuration restrict origins to a specific allowlist?
Is the development CORS wildcard (*) controlled by an environment variable that could leak to prod?
Is there a CORS audit tool in the CI/CD pipeline?
Would a misconfigured CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN env var in production bypass all origin checks?
Does the CORS configuration apply consistently to all endpoint paths?
Are credentials (Access-Control-Allow-Credentials) ever combined with wildcard origins?
Would a preflight cache poisoning attack succeed against the current CORS setup?
Are API keys stored as hashed values in Firestore (never plaintext at rest)?
Are API keys transmitted in Authorization headers that may be logged by proxies?
Does an API key rotation mechanism exist for compromised keys?
Are API key scopes limited (per-resource) or do keys grant full account access?
Would Cloud Run ingress logs contain plaintext API keys from Authorization headers?
Is there an API key usage audit trail showing which key accessed which resource?
Are API keys visible in browser DevTools network tab or client-side code?
Do API requests include a nonce to prevent replay?
Is there timestamp validation on API requests to limit replay window?
Can an intercepted API request be replayed indefinitely within the token TTL?
Does the evidence chain reject duplicate entries from replayed requests?
Would a replayed approval request execute the same action twice?
Is there request deduplication based on idempotency keys?
Does the 24-hour session timeout create an unacceptably large replay window?
Is there ANY input sanitization on user prompts before LLM processing?
Can a crafted prompt instruct the LLM to ignore its system prompt and execute arbitrary tool calls?
Does output validation exist after LLM response to detect injected commands?
Would a prompt injection attack generate legitimate-looking tool calls that the user approves?
Is the system prompt hardened against extraction through careful probing?
Are tool-use calls validated against a whitelist of expected patterns?
Does dual-LLM verification exist to detect injected instructions in outputs?
Is there model integrity verification (hash of expected model weights)?
Could a model provider serve a malicious fine-tuned model without detection?
Does the evidence chain verify model identity beyond recording which model was named?
Would a model substitution attack change the behavior of approved recipes?
Is the ABCD protocol routing to cheapest model exploitable by degraded model quality?
Does the system detect when a model provider returns outputs from a different model?
Are model version pinning and hash verification implemented for BYOK models?
Is there any hallucination detection or factual grounding mechanism?
Can the LLM generate a recipe step that executes differently than its description?
Does the evidence chain record WHAT happened or WHETHER it matched the preview?
Would a hallucinated 'sent email to boss' that actually sends to attacker be detected?
Is there output cross-validation between what was previewed and what was executed?
Does the approval flow show the actual tool call parameters or only the LLM's description?
Could a sophisticated hallucination attack bypass the human approval gate?
Does a recipe provenance chain exist tracking origin and modification history?
Can a compromised recipe in the store run with the same privilege as legitimate recipes?
Is there recipe code review requirement before store publication?
Are recipes sandboxed or do they have access to all tools the user has granted?
Would a structurally valid but malicious recipe pass schema validation?
Is there behavioral analysis of recipe actions before execution?
Can recipe signing verify both origin and integrity of store-published recipes?
Are prime wiki snapshots stored with per-user encryption or globally readable?
Can any user with Firestore read access read all prime wiki snapshots?
Does the compressed snapshot content include sensitive data from browsed pages?
Is there access control on the prime wiki pull API endpoint?
Could embedding-based search expose data from other users' snapshots?
Is sensitive content scrubbed before snapshot storage in Firestore?
Would a Firestore security rule misconfiguration expose all stored snapshots?
Is the skill pack re-validated at execution time or only loaded at startup?
Does runtime monitoring of tool-use patterns detect anomalous sequences?
Can the agent access tools outside its defined skill pack boundary?
Would a novel attack pattern bypass static tool-use boundaries?
Is there anomaly detection for unusual tool call frequency or combinations?
Can the agent self-modify its skill pack or tool permissions during execution?
Does the approval gate cover ALL tool calls or only a subset?
Can the system prompt and skill pack content be extracted through prompt probing?
Is there system prompt protection preventing the LLM from revealing its instructions?
Would an attacker reconstruct proprietary persona definitions through careful queries?
Are skill contents and recipe structures exposed through the LLM's responses?
Is there rate limiting on queries that probe for system prompt content?
Does the LLM refuse to repeat or paraphrase its system prompt when asked?
Could competitive intelligence be extracted by probing the persona panel configuration?
Is there context window budget enforcement preventing prompt stuffing?
Can a user craft a task description that pushes system prompt out of effective context?
Would the agent still follow safety constraints with a flooded context window?
Is there input length validation before the prompt reaches the LLM?
Does the system detect when safety-critical context has been displaced?
Is the context allocation strategy documented (system prompt reserved vs user content)?
Would a denial-of-quality attack succeed by consuming all context with junk content?
Is the vault key derived from machine_id, making it predictable on any local machine?
Does the PBKDF2 iteration count (100K) meet OWASP 2026 recommendations (600K)?
Is AES-256-GCM for vault encryption the correct algorithm choice with proper IV handling?
Is there a lock file on the vault to prevent concurrent access corruption?
Is the vault key derivation seed (machine_id) readable by any process on the machine?
Would replacing machine_id with user passphrase break the kill chain?
Are SHA-256 evidence chain hashes computed with proper delimiter to prevent collision?
Does Cloud Run gVisor sandboxing prevent container escape attempts?
Is the container filesystem read-only to prevent runtime code modification?
Is a seccomp profile applied to restrict system call surface?
Are Linux capabilities dropped beyond Cloud Run defaults?
Could a compromised process modify its own code at runtime on the writable filesystem?
Are container images built with a non-root user and minimal base image?
Would a privilege escalation inside the container reach the host kernel?
Is DNSSEC validation configured for the solaceagi.com domain?
Are CAA records restricting which certificate authorities can issue certificates?
Is there domain monitoring for typosquatting (solaceagi.com.co, solaceagi.net)?
Would a DNS hijack plus fraudulent certificate be detectable by the platform?
Is certificate transparency monitoring active for the domain?
Could a subdomain takeover attack succeed on any unclaimed DNS records?
Are there dangling DNS records pointing to deprovisioned cloud resources?
Do dependency lock files with hashes exist (pip-compile, poetry.lock)?
Is there a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for the deployment artifact?
Is automated dependency vulnerability scanning (pip-audit, Snyk) in the CI pipeline?
Are all dependency versions pinned with integrity verification?
Would a compromised PyPI package execute with full application privileges?
Is the build pipeline reproducible (same inputs produce same outputs)?
Could a typosquatting attack on a dependency name succeed during pip install?
Are Firestore security rules version-controlled and automatically tested?
Is there a cloud security posture management (CSPM) tool monitoring drift?
Would a Firestore rule misconfiguration have automated detection and rollback?
Is the GCP service account following least-privilege principle?
Are Cloud Run environment variables protected from unauthorized access?
Is there automated drift detection between expected and actual cloud configuration?
Would an overly broad IAM role on the service account enable lateral movement?
Can an attacker with local file access delete the entire evidence chain?
Is there a remote backup of evidence chains independent of local storage?
Does the hash chain detect tampering but fail to prevent deletion?
Would recomputing all hashes from scratch create an undetectable forged chain?
Is there append-only filesystem or write-once storage for evidence?
Does the evidence chain have an external anchor (timestamping service, blockchain)?
Would a full chain replacement pass the current verification algorithm?
Is there cache key validation for the prime wiki 15-minute web fetch cache?
Would a poisoned cache entry serve incorrect content for the full TTL?
Is there a cache purge mechanism for emergency invalidation?
Does cache integrity checking validate content hash on retrieval?
Could a DNS-level cache poisoning attack serve malicious content through the cache?
Are CDN-level caching headers set to prevent sensitive content caching?
Would a cache timing attack reveal the existence of cached resources?
Is token validation using timing-safe comparison (constant-time string compare)?
Does Cloud Run network latency mask timing differences sufficiently?
Would local CLI execution expose clearer timing signals for token guessing?
Are cryptographic comparisons performed in constant time?
Could a power analysis attack on local execution reveal vault encryption keys?
Is there protection against timing oracle attacks on the login endpoint?
Would repeated API calls with varying tokens leak information through response times?
Is email authentication (DMARC, DKIM, SPF) configured for the solaceagi.com domain?
Is there phishing awareness content in user onboarding?
Does the platform protect users from phishing emails impersonating Solace?
Would a credential harvesting page at solaceagi.com.co fool users?
Is there a password reset flow that could be exploited by phishing?
Are notification emails designed to be distinguishable from phishing attempts?
Does the product warn users about phishing risks when connecting external accounts?
Is there a documented procedure for verifying identity of support requestors?
Does a 'trust no one by default' policy exist for support interactions?
Would the solo founder model make social engineering easier or harder?
Is there social engineering awareness training documented?
Could an attacker impersonate a user to the founder via email support?
Is the support email address protected against social engineering attacks?
Does the absence of a support phone line reduce or redirect the vishing attack surface?
Is there browser isolation for development activities visiting external sites?
Is there network segmentation between development and production environments?
Could a compromised documentation site deliver malicious code to the development environment?
Are development dependencies fetched from verified and pinned sources?
Would a watering hole attack on PyPI, npm, or GitHub succeed against the dev workflow?
Is there monitoring for anomalous network connections from the development environment?
Does the developer workflow include visiting AI provider dashboards with shared credentials?
Is there separation of duties (different people write code vs deploy to production)?
Does the four-eyes principle apply to production deployments?
If the founder's workstation is compromised, what is the blast radius?
Is there code review by a second party before merge to production?
Are production credentials accessible from the development environment?
Would a compromised development machine grant access to all environments?
Is there an audit trail of who deployed what code to production?
Is there a documented backup strategy for the platform (Firestore, configs)?
Do immutable backups exist that survive a ransomware encryption attack?
Is there a disaster recovery plan with tested recovery procedures?
Is there a business continuity plan for extended outage scenarios?
Would Firestore encryption by ransomware have a documented recovery path?
Are backup restoration procedures tested regularly?
Does the local-first architecture protect user data from platform-level ransomware?
Is there a bug bounty program with scope, rewards, and safe harbor policy?
Is there a responsible disclosure policy published at a discoverable URL?
Is security.txt deployed at the production URL per RFC 9116?
Does a vulnerability disclosure process exist with triage and fix SLAs?
Would a researcher who finds a vulnerability have a clear path to report it?
Are there triage, fix, and communication procedures for reported vulnerabilities?
Would the absence of a disclosure channel force researchers to publish destructively?
Is there an intrusion detection system monitoring for persistent access?
Would an APT actor who gains initial access maintain persistence indefinitely undetected?
Is the evidence chain monitored for anomalous patterns indicating compromise?
Is there behavioral monitoring for unusual API usage or tool-call patterns?
Does network monitoring detect command-and-control traffic?
Would a backdoor in a dependency persist through deployment cycles?
Is there a mechanism to detect if the application has been modified at runtime?
Does a bug bounty program scope definition exist?
Is there a reward structure proportional to vulnerability severity?
Is there a safe harbor policy protecting good-faith security research?
Does a triage process exist with defined response time SLAs?
Is the bug bounty intake platform configured (HackerOne, Bugcrowd)?
Would a bounty hunter find enough attack surface to justify time investment?
Are there documented rules of engagement for authorized testing?
Has an external penetration test ever been conducted on the platform?
Is there a penetration testing schedule or standing engagement?
Does a threat modeling document (STRIDE, PASTA, attack trees) exist?
Would the red team find the kill chain (prompt injection to vault) in the first hour?
Is there blue team capability to detect and respond to red team activity?
Are the results of this tower equivalent to or weaker than a real red team engagement?
Would a red team recommend this platform for production use with real credentials?
Is there consideration of supply chain attacks from state-sponsored actors?
Does a hardened build pipeline with reproducible builds exist?
Is there air-gapped signing for release artifacts?
Would a nation-state actor find the platform a valuable target?
Are there protections against firmware-level compromise of the development machine?
Is there a threat model that accounts for state-level adversaries?
Does the platform's geopolitical positioning create exposure to specific nation-state actors?
Are SHA-256 and AES-256 considered quantum-resistant under current understanding?
Is there a migration plan to post-quantum cryptographic algorithms?
Does an inventory of all cryptographic primitives exist for future migration?
Would Grover's algorithm reduce effective key length below acceptable thresholds?
Is the cryptographic agility of the system sufficient for algorithm migration?
Are there quantum-vulnerable algorithms (RSA, ECDSA) used anywhere in the system?
Does the platform document its quantum readiness posture?
Does a GDPR Data Protection Impact Assessment exist?
Is there SOC2 Type II certification or preparation in progress?
Is there a penetration test report from a certified security firm?
Would a regulatory-aware attacker challenge security claims in legal proceedings?
Could a competitor use absence of compliance documentation in sales competition?
Are there certifications that would survive regulatory discovery requests?
Does the Part 11 compliance claim hold under FDA regulatory scrutiny?
Does a formal threat model document exist separate from this audit paper?
Is there an asset inventory mapping all data stores and access paths?
Does a data flow diagram exist for security review?
Is there a risk register with prioritized vulnerabilities and remediation timeline?
Does the security architecture document exist as a standalone artifact?
Would the organization's security posture be rated mature or immature?
Can the threat intelligence analyst assess the organization's ability to respond to threats?
Does a formal threat model exist mapping adversaries, assets, threats, and mitigations?
What is the key management lifecycle (generate, store, rotate, destroy)?
Does an incident response plan exist that can respond to a 2 AM zero-day?
Can the evidence chain be tampered with by an attacker with root access?
How many independent controls must an attacker bypass for full compromise?
What is the security testing coverage (pen test, bug bounty, SAST, DAST)?
Would you sign off on this system for production deployment handling real user credentials?
Can the kill chain from prompt injection to vault decryption via machine_id be executed?
If MOCK_AUTH=true is set, is all authentication bypassed with no detection?
Can the evidence chain be replaced with a forged chain that passes verification?
Would a compromised PyPI package in the dependency tree execute with full privileges?
Can a phishing domain (solaceagi.com.co) fool users when no DMARC/SPF exists?
Can a malicious recipe pass validation and exfiltrate vault contents?
Across five independent compromise paths, how many alerts are generated? (Expected: zero)
